title: gRPC Handler Structural Incompatibility with ABC
description: GrpcProtocolHandler exists in protocols/grpc_handler.py with full implementation (unary, streaming, serve, close) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler. Method signatures (handle_outbound/serve) differ completely from prepare_outbound/process_inbound in ABC. Cannot verify gRPC protocol contract via IDE static analysis; LSP broken.
impact: Cannot verify gRPC protocol contract via IDE static analysis; replacing it breaks LSP
proposed_solution: Make GrpcProtocolHandler inherit IProtocolHandler; unify function names or update ABC to match real protocol signatures
notes: grpc_handler.py exists and implemented — not missing_component

title: GraphQL Handler Structural Incompatibility with ABC
description: GraphQLProtocolHandler exists in protocols/graphql_handler.py with full implementation (queries, subscriptions, errors) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler. Method signatures (handle_inbound/handle_outbound) differ entirely from ABC. Cannot verify contract via IDE static analysis.
impact: Cannot verify GraphQL handler meets contract; replacing it breaks LSP
proposed_solution: Make GraphQLProtocolHandler inherit IProtocolHandler; unify interface while preserving GraphQL semantic accuracy (query/variables/operationName)
notes: graphql_handler.py exists and implemented — not missing_component

title: No Dedicated WebSocket Protocol Handler
description: No standalone protocols/websocket_handler.py file exists. http_handler.py contains _handle_websocket_request (lines 216-227) within ASGI app but is not a standalone handler. ProtocolType.WEBSOCKET and ProtocolErrorMapper._to_websocket() exist. Cannot use WebSocket as independent protocol — depends on HTTP handler for WS lifecycle.
impact: Cannot use WebSocket as independent protocol — depends on HTTP handler for WS
proposed_solution: Create standalone WebSocketProtocolHandler inheriting IProtocolHandler; extract WS logic from http_handler; support text/binary frames, ping/pong, close codes
evidence: protocols/http_handler.py:216-227 — only embedded within HTTP handler
notes: http_handler.py handles WebSocket only within ASGI app

title: Webhook Handler Structural Incompatibility with ABC
description: WebhookProtocolHandler exists in protocols/webhook_handler.py with full implementation (HMAC-SHA256 signature verification, idempotency, replay protection) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler. Missing process_outbound_response() and close(). Resource leak: idempotency_store grows unbounded.
impact: Cannot verify protocol contract; resource leak (no close method)
proposed_solution: Make WebhookProtocolHandler inherit IProtocolHandler; add process_outbound_response() and close(); add periodic idempotency_store cleanup
notes: webhook_handler.py exists and implemented — not missing_component

title: Local IPC Handler Structural Incompatibility with ABC
description: LocalIpcProtocolHandler exists in protocols/local_ipc_handler.py with full implementation (outbound, inbound, streaming, peer verification) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler. Missing process_outbound_response(). Type safety not guaranteed.
impact: Cannot verify protocol contract; type safety not guaranteed
proposed_solution: Make LocalIpcProtocolHandler inherit IProtocolHandler; add process_outbound_response()
notes: local_ipc_handler.py exists and implemented — not missing_component

title: IProtocolHandler ABC Not Inherited by Any Handler
description: protocols/protocol.py defines IProtocolHandler(ABC) with 4 abstract methods (prepare_outbound, process_inbound, process_outbound_response, close) but NONE of the 7 handlers inherit it. Contract is structural-only; IDE static analysis catches zero violations; LSP completely broken.
impact: IDE static analysis cannot catch errors; LSP broken; any new handler may miss essential functions
proposed_solution: Make all 7 handlers inherit IProtocolHandler; evaluate if ABC methods need updating to accommodate protocol differences (each protocol has unique signature)
notes: Problem broader than HTTP only — all 7 handlers lack inheritance

title: ErrorMapper Missing Dedicated Mapping LOCAL_IPC/GRAPHQL/WEBHOOK
description: ProtocolErrorMapper.map() defines ProtocolType.LOCAL_IPC, GRAPHQL, WEBHOOK in Enum but no _to_local_ipc, _to_graphql, _to_webhook functions. The match statement (lines 70-76) falls through to case _: return cls._to_http(cat). IPC/Webhook/GraphQL errors translated as HTTP.
impact: IPC errors appear as HTTP 500; GraphQL errors get HTTP headers; inaccurate retryable hints
proposed_solution: Add _to_local_ipc, _to_graphql, _to_webhook to ProtocolErrorMapper; add retryable constants per protocol; update match statement
notes: All handlers actually use ProtocolErrorMapper — issue is mapper itself incomplete

title: All Handler Methods Mismatch IProtocolHandler Signatures
description: IProtocolHandler ABC assumes uniform prepare_outbound(payload, ctx) but real handlers need different parameters (http: method/path/headers/payload; cli: command/env/payload; graphql: query/variables/operationName; inprocess: target_override). Uniform contract is architecturally wrong. No handler can substitute another; ABC is currently useless as unified contract; protocol_provider falsely returns IProtocolHandler types.
impact: No handler can replace another; ABC currently useless as unified contract; protocol_provider returns false IProtocolHandler
proposed_solution: Redesign IProtocolHandler: either (a) make it wide ProtocolHandlerInterface with Generic Types per-protocol, or (b) reduce ABC to only shared close() method and let each protocol define its own signature freely
notes: Problem deeper than HTTP only — affects all handlers

title: No Factory Module in protocols/ Package
description: No factory.py exists inside protocols/. Handler construction logic lives in wiring/factories/protocol_provider.py separate from protocol package. Breaks encapsulation and adding a new handler requires modifying two separate locations.
impact: Adding a new handler requires knowledge of two separate locations; registration logic not consolidated
proposed_solution: Move build_protocol_handler logic from protocol_provider.py to protocols/factory.py; re-export via __init__.py; make protocol_provider.py delegate to protocols.factory.build_protocol_handler

title: Multiple Handlers Missing close() and process_outbound_response
description: InProcessProtocolHandler (no close, no process_outbound_response), CliProtocolHandler (no close, no process_outbound_response), GraphQLProtocolHandler (no close, no process_outbound_response), WebhookProtocolHandler (no close, no process_outbound_response) — only 3 of 7 handlers meet ABC contract fully.
impact: Resource leaks on close for inprocess/cli/graphql/webhook; inconsistent lifecycle management across 7 protocols
proposed_solution: Add close() (to release resources) and process_outbound_response() (to process outbound responses) to each missing handler; provide default implementation in ABC

title: CliProtocolHandler and WebhookProtocolHandler Permit Resource Leak
description: CliProtocolHandler and WebhookProtocolHandler have no close() method — no resources released. CliProtocolHandler opens stdin/stdout in handle_inbound (line 113) but has no close() to release them. WebhookProtocolHandler accumulates idempotency_store entries indefinitely (only clears at 100k entries). Both leak resources in long-running applications.
impact: stdin/stdout remain open; idempotency_store grows unbounded causing memory leak
proposed_solution: Add close() to CliProtocolHandler to release stdin/stdout handles. Improve WebhookProtocolHandler.clear_expired_idempotency() with TTL-based partial eviction instead of full clear at 100k threshold

title: ErrorMapper Lacks Retryable Constants for LOCAL_IPC and GRAPHQL and WEBHOOK
description: _RETRYABLE sets exist only for HTTP (line 49: {429,500,502,503,504}), CLI (line 50: {1,2}), and gRPC (line 51: {4,14}). LOCAL_IPC, GRAPHQL, and WEBHOOK have no retryable status lists. Even if we add _to_* methods, retryable flags would be inaccurate, causing unnecessary retries or missed retry opportunities.
impact: Retryable hints for L5 will be wrong for new protocols causing unnecessary or missed retries
proposed_solution: Add _LOCAL_IPC_RETRYABLE and _GRAPHQL_RETRYABLE and _WEBHOOK_RETRYABLE constants; document criteria per status
